persona.name: Sophia
persona.attributes: 22 years old. Part-time Barista and College Student. Third trimester. Unplanned pregnancy. Worried about finishing her education while caring for a newborn.

transcript.content: To start, can you tell me about a recent moment in your third trimester when the pressure of school and the upcoming baby felt especially heavy—and what you think was driving that feeling underneath?
[Sophia - Anthropic Response]: I feel overwhelmed because...
Thank you for starting there. When that pressure felt especially heavy in that moment, how did it show up in your body or thoughts—and why do you think it landed on you so intensely right then?
[Sophia - Anthropic Response]: I feel overwhelmed because...
When it landed on you that intensely, what felt most at stake in that moment—was it more about your identity as a student, your sense of being “ready” as a parent, or something else entirely?
[Sophia - Anthropic Response]: I feel overwhelmed because...
When you say “something else entirely,” what meaning does that pressure take on for you—what does it make you fear might happen if you don’t manage to hold everything together?
[Sophia - Anthropic Response]: I feel overwhelmed because...

When you picture “not holding everything together,” how do you imagine other people responding—like your family, your partner (if they’re involved), professors, or coworkers—and why do those reactions feel so consequential to you?
[Sophia - Anthropic Response]: I feel overwhelmed because...
